titleStmt.title: SII 40.316A: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 316A. (A.R. No. B. 364 of 1933.) Kōḍūru, Jammalamadugu Taluk, Cuddapah District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a stone lying near the Chennakēśava temple.
This is dated Śaka 1471, Kīlaka, Bhādrapada, śu. 14.

Records the gift of 4(1/2) Varāhas of Dommari Pannu collected from the village of Kōḍūru in Gutti-sīma by some members of the Dommari community, for providing offerings and burning a perpetual lamp to the god Channarāya of the village for the merit of their community when mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Aḷiya Rāmappayyadēva-Mahārāja was ruling the region.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu
svasti śrī jayādbhu
daya śālivāhana
śakava¨rṣaṁbulu
1471 Agunēṭi kīla
ka saṁvatsara bhādrapa
da śu 14 lu śrīmanma
mahārājādhirāja
rājaparamēśvara śrī
vīrapratāpa śrī satāsi
vadēva mahārāyalu
gāru vidyānagaramaṁ
du vajrasiṁhvāsanā
rūḍhuṁḍai pṛthvīsā
mrājyaṁ śēyucuṁ
ḍaṁgānu śrī¨rmamahā
maṁḍalēśvara Aḷaya
rāmappayyadēva mahā
rājulugāru pāli
ṁcci yiyyavadhariṁ
cina guttisīmalō
pennabaḍigrāmaṁ
kōḍūri grā

śrīmatu Akilāṁ
ḍakōṭi braṁhmāṁḍa
nāyaka vēdavēdāṁtta
vēdya purāṇa puruṣō
ttamuṁḍaina śrī koḍū
ri cennarāyani sva
dāsānudāsulaina
doṁmmari rāmacaṁdṛni
tirumalarāyani ta
mmuṁḍu sirigiri reḍi ko
ḍukulu mu¨rddayya peddi
nēḍu jalikiboktiṁ ni
reṁḍu nallaṁgaṁnnaseṭṭi
tipparājuku vōṭa
dinaḷa kōḍūri cenna
rāyaniki dhāravōsi
yiccina dha¨rmmaśāsana
ṁmma kramameṭlannanu
cennakēśaruni dīpārā
dhana Amṛtapaḷlakunu
yī kōḍūrigrāmaṁ
nu vaccina doṁmmari pa
nnu vaga 479 māyiru
vainālugu kulālavā
riki puṇyaṁgānu dhāra
vostimi yīdhaṁ¨rmmā
naku yavarunu tappi
nānu tamatallidaṁḍru
lanu
lines are damaged
jāyanamaḥ

ṁdoṁmmarivariki koḍukulu Enasa koḍukulu Anitama yiruvainālgu kulāla
vārikini puṇyaṁgānu dhāravostimi yiccina dha¨rmmaśāsanaṁ .
svadattādviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ paradattāpaharaṇa svadattaṁ niṣphālaṁ bhavēt śrī
Digital edition of SII 40.316A by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
